title: NFL closes its review of the Stefon Diggs allegations, saying there’s not enough evidence to punish him
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots The decision comes after Diggs was found not guilty of assault and strangulation charges last month.
Stefon Diggs AP Photo/Julio Cortez The NFL has decided that there’s not enough evidence to prove that Stefon Diggs violated its personal conduct policy.
The league’s review of the situation is now closed, according to multiple reports.

title: Haiti’s Frantzdy Pierrot grew up in Melrose. For him, the World Cup is a homecoming.

Frantzdy Pierrot at the Massachusetts State House with Gov.

title: Registration doesn’t guarantee admission to World Cup fan fest in Boston, Wu says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: World Cup The World Cup fan festival on City Hall Plaza maxed out registrations for its first two days.
Pedestrians walk past preparations for the FIFA Fan Festival outside Boston City Hall.
Erin Clark/Boston Globe As soccer fans from around the world arrive in Boston to celebrate the World Cup, there is high demand for some official watch parties hosted by the city.

title: Everything you need to know about Sail Boston 2026
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Local News The tall ships will be in Boston July 11-16.
Tall ships arrived in Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 2000.
John Tlumacki/Globe staff Dozens of tall ships will glide into Boston Harbor in July for Sail Boston 2026, one of the largest gatherings of tall ships in America in decades.
